summary: Handle server command responses without "ok" field
add allowDiskUse:true to the top-level of an aggregate command
Commands sent to mongos sometimes break when sending $readPreference
please update description of 'fsync' write concern flag
description:
Since your driver's documentation (e.g. [here|http://api.mongodb.org/perl/current/MongoDB/Database.html]) describes the 'fsync' write concern flag, can you please update its description so that it accurately describes the current behavior, which is:

# 'fsync' is identical to 'j' if mongod is running with a journal (today, 99% of users should be here)
# 'fsync' requests that the write be sync()'d to disk only if mongod is not running with a journal
# 'fsync' cannot be used simultaneously with the 'j' flag

The point is that:
- 'j' means 'sync via the journal if present, or error if not, or if 'fsync' is also set'
- 'fsync' means 'sync via the journal if present, or sync via msync() if there is no journal, and error if 'j' is also set'

Additionally, could you please mark the 'fsync' flag as "Deprecated; do not use", following this commit [here|https://github.com/mongodb/docs/commit/75c648730b54a920cf85372fd27ab8600b67b794]

Please see SERVER-11399 for background.

Thanks!

summary: MongoClient hogs memory
description:
(possibly related to [PERL-267])

Memory consumption of the perl driver prevents us from upgrading.

The memory consumption has been steadily increasing during runtime ever since the introduction of MongoClient.pm (0.502.0).

We haven't been able to track down the exact source of the problem, but the attached script shows the difference in behavior with versions pre and post 0.501.1.

e.g.

{noformat}
PERL5LIB=/home/jan.anderssen/mongo-driver-test/0.501.1 perl mongo-test.pl
0.501.1
31732
using MongoDB::Connection
iteration 1: VmRSS 0 --> 21944 (21944)
... (stays that way)
{noformat}

in contrast to

{noformat}
PERL5LIB=/home/jan.anderssen/mongo-driver-test/0.502.0 perl mongo-test.pl 0.502.0
30707
using MongoDB::MongoClient
iteration 1: VmRSS 0 --> 22120 (22120)
iteration 220: VmRSS 22120 --> 22124 (4)
iteration 317: VmRSS 22124 --> 22128 (4)
iteration 389: VmRSS 22128 --> 22132 (4)
iteration 423: VmRSS 22132 --> 22136 (4)
iteration 445: VmRSS 22136 --> 22140 (4)
(keeps increasing)
{noformat}

or

{noformat}
PERL5LIB=/home/jan.anderssen/mongo-driver-test/0.702.2 perl mongo-test.pl  
0.702.2
2902
using MongoDB::MongoClient
iteration 1: VmRSS 0 --> 22924 (22924)
iteration 94: VmRSS 22924 --> 22928 (4)
iteration 96: VmRSS 22928 --> 22932 (4)
iteration 152: VmRSS 22932 --> 22936 (4)
iteration 166: VmRSS 22936 --> 22940 (4)
iteration 183: VmRSS 22940 --> 22944 (4)
iteration 208: VmRSS 22944 --> 22952 (8)
iteration 264: VmRSS 22952 --> 22956 (4)
iteration 272: VmRSS 22956 --> 22960 (4)
iteration 321: VmRSS 22960 --> 22964 (4)
{noformat}

summary: MongoDB Client Memory Leak when Inserting records - sample code included
description:
If you insert records in a loop, the process ram usage grows forever. There seems be a memory leak in the MongoDB PERL Lib. Attached is the simple version of trivial perl script which will eat all the memory on the local machine.

If you add "Devel::Leak" you can see that MogoDB is allocating new memory in every call to collection->save and not releasing it.

This makes MongoDB very dangerous to use with long-running perl process.

CPAN tells me I am using version 0.702.2 of MongoDB. BUT That version is not available in the version list above, so I selected  0.702.1.

This is perl 5, version 14, subversion 2 (v5.14.2) built for x86_64-linux-gnu-thread-multi.

summary: MongoDB::MongoClient::recv incorrectly documented
description: Documented as taking a hashref and returning a cursor when in fact it takes a cursor and has no return.

summary: SASL connection recursion within _sasl_start method
description: When {{auto_reconnect}} is on and SASL authentication is being used, if a mongod is not available, the connect method will enter an infinite recursion.

summary: Resource leak in mongo_link.c
description:
Coverity identified a resource leak in non_ssl_connect: [https://coverity.mongodb.com/reports.htm#v11858/p10009/fileInstanceId=3828075&defectInstanceId=1014751&mergedDefectId=10729&eventId=1014751-5]

This may be related to other connection/leak tickets, but I haven't investigated that yet.

summary: Collection find_and_modify may use error string as document (croaks when using strict)
description:
Certain error conditions - I found it when testing a duplicate value in a unique-indexed field - will cause MongoDB::Collection's find_and_modify to behave incorrectly, and croak if strict is being used.

The issue begins on line 236, where the result of db->run_command is treated as a document (i.e. a hash ref).  But in the error case, this return value is a string instead.

The result should be examined more carefully before trying to use as a hash ref, or run_command should be prohibited from returning a raw string.

summary: add persistent connections
description: useful for mod_perl

summary: perl mongodb - moose driver Not inlining a constructor for Moose::Meta::Method::Accessor
description:
Not inlining a constructor for Moose::Meta::Method::Accessor since it defines its own constructor.
If you are certain you don't need to inline your constructor, specify inline_constructor => 0 in your call to Moose::Meta::Method::Accessor->meta->make_immutable

summary: bulk object initialization methods not documented in Collection class
description:
The Collection class doesn't document the bulk write operation initializers.

(They are shown in the SYNOPSIS section for the BulkWrite class.)

summary: find() broken under read preference for non-hash query types
description:
Several internal methods called by {{find}} assume the query is a hashref and can break if the query is an arrayref or IxHash.

Because of hash randomization we need to support arrayrefs or IxHash for command queries. Any incoming query parameter should be changed to an IxHash immediately and the internal functions modified to match.

summary: Detect test mongod running in auth mode and skip testing
description:
See failures here: http://www.cpantesters.org/cpan/report/b3b48306-e5e3-11e3-bc00-c421527115a9

Spinning up our own test mongod rather than relying on system default one will also address this issue.

summary: Fix createIndexes so error code 13390 is handled correctly.
RS/Shard unit tests not running
description:
The internal test libraries MongoDBTest::ShardedCluster and MongoDB::Test::ReplicaSet are fragile, requiring specific paths, etc.  They are not run by default because they require setting particularly environment variables.

The test strategy and these test libraries need to be reconsidered to be more general.  They should be made to run regularly in the CI environment.

summary: Have tests report on version/config of MongoDB used for testing
description: Tests should include MongoDB details in diagnostic note so that pasted test output in bug reports can save us asking bug reporter about the version of MongoDB, etc.

summary: Instructions for installing Perl Driver for Amazon linux failing
description:
So, I'm not much of a PERL guy so I apologize if I use all the wrong words here.  

The instructions for installing the Perl Driver for linux are not currently working on Amazon Linux
http://docs.mongodb.org/ecosystem/drivers/perl/

neither the CPAN nor the non-CPAN  version of the instructions are succeeding.

An associate at a different organization indicated his build was failing got farther than me and was able to get dzil running via a different path than the instructions.  His notes:
* Spent several hours in the CPAN perl dependency swamp and appear to have a running version of dzil via Dist:Zilla
*Alas the "dzil authordeps | sudo cpanm" step runs into the missing package documented here:
http://cpantesters.org/cpan/report/22a4404c-c5f7-11e3-8510-86b416100da4

Could instructions or a workaround for installing the perl driver be given?

summary: Test t/dbref.t ok only in the first installation
description:
If you need to reinstall the module or install it for new user - the test do not pass:

{quote}
t/dbref.t ................ 1/28 E11000 duplicate key error index: test.some_coll.$_id_  dup key: { : 123 } at t/dbref.t line 91.
\# Looks like you planned 28 tests but ran 18.
\# Looks like your test exited with 115 just after 18.
t/dbref.t ................ Dubious, test returned 115 (wstat 29440, 0x7300)
{quote}

But if in db 'test' drop collection 'some_coll' - the test pass again (one time)

summary: 'safe => 1' does not work for 'Perl5.8.8-i686-linux-thread-multi' on 'update' commands
description:
Running the Perl driver with Perl 5.8.8/multi-threaded on 32bits/Linux does not work.
The same test on single-threaded Linux or Solaris is working fine.

Script is:
===========

{code:perl}
my $version = $MongoDB::VERSION;
print("MongoDB driver version: $version\n");
print(join("\n", @INC));
my $conn = MongoDB::MongoClient->new(host => 'localhost');
my $coll = $conn->get_database('test')->get_collection('perl');
# 'insert' works
my $id = $coll->insert({"version" => $version}, {safe => 1});
# 'update' fails
$coll->update({"_id" => $id}, {"cmd" => "update"}, {safe => 1});
{code}

=======
Output and debug info
=======
{noformat}
sjc-scmtest-v01:212> ./mongodb_driver_issue.pl

summary: Add ipv6 support
Ver. 0.45 is NOT thread safe
description:
The short version of this description is that the 0.45 driver is NOT thread safe despite it being claimed in the documentation.

We have used the MongoDB PERL driver for about 2 years in a setup which basically receives JSON documents using Apache/mod_perl and saves them on a single MongoDB instance. We handle many thousands of documents per minute.
We have observed a lot of leaked scalars and segmentation faults during operation, which we could sort of live with hoping the driver would get better with time (reported in https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/PERL-122).

Yesterday we expanded our setup to having two different MongoDB hosts to cope with more clients.
We immediately observed that a few documents randomly landed on the wrong host (we make separate connections for each save). 
The connections are getting MIXED in the threads!

Today we tried going for just one thread per process in Apache and that solved both the leaking and the connection mixing.
We can only conclude that the driver in NOT thread safe.

I'll be happy to supply more information about our setup and code if needed.

summary: Leak of connections on some conditions
possible error in xs/OID.xs
description:
(via email from michael.chalfant@gmail.com)

I was evaluating MongoDB-0.45 for possible use.
In the review, I noticed a logical error in xs/OID.xs

This code would be invoked if the argument to _build_value: oid_sv is not NULL.

Line 33:
Copy(oid, SvPVX(oid_sv), 24, char);

The 1st two arguments to the macro are transposed. The source is the 1st argument and the destination is the 2nd.
Since oid was predeclared but not explicitly initialized there will be unpredictable content returned from _build_value.

I'm not certain this branch is triggered during normal operation of MongoDB.

Also, perhaps related, in t/bson.t line 65 uses an undocumented form of the MongoDB::OID constructor:

"_id" => MongoDB::OID->new("49b6d9fb17330414a0c63101")

When I first read this code, I was expecting the above to create a MongoDB::OID object with the value given in the argument.
In practice, this acts as if there were no arguments to the constructor, and a new id is generated via _build_value and assigned to the object.
This allows the test script to pass.

Again, I doubt these are particularly relevant to the overall function and use of the driver, but thought you would want to be informed.
In any event, congratulations on a well-built and well-documented distribution.

summary: Perl Driver divide by -1 causes early exit
description:
receiving this message on running an expected to be long running map reduce via perl , "can't get db response, not connected".

// using 

    CPAN_USERID  KRISTINA (Kristina Chodorow <k.chodorow@gmail.com>)
    CPAN_VERSION 0.702001
    CPAN_FILE    F/FR/FRIEDO/MongoDB-0.702.1.tar.gz
    UPLOAD_DATE  2013-08-12
    MANPAGE      MongoDB - A MongoDB Driver for Perl
    INST_FILE    /usr/local/lib/perl/5.14.2/MongoDB.pm
    INST_VERSION 0.702.1


// my $conn = MongoDB::Connection->new( host => $host , query_timeout => -1);
// had to change line 35 of mongo_link.c to remove division of timeout, if timeout is set at -1.


ln:35   tv.tv_sec = timeout ; /* / 1000; */


another approach aside by commenting out the 1000 would probably be better if its to be taken into account to divide by 1000 for some reason.  this seems to have been an issue a few years ago that crept up again recently.  unless setting the timeout to -1 is no longer a valid option. 

https://groups.google.com/forum/#!msg/mongodb-user/BxHM3cM4q5Q/3Kd21dNnVHEJ

https://groups.google.com/forum/?fromgroups=#!topic/mongodb-user/4TDQKoo0Cd4

https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/PERL-196

summary: Implement cursor batchSize
description: Following Scott's poke for PHP-284, he suggested that I filed the same request also for the other drivers. The PHP docs now have the full explanation (with some additional warnings regarding batchSize of 1): http://docs.php.net/manual/en/mongocursor.batchsize.php
